document.source_title: WHO’s response to hantavirus cases linked to a cruise ship
document.author: Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus
document.summary: WHO is responding to a cluster of hantavirus cases linked to the cruise ship MV Hondius, with eight cases reported, including three deaths.

claims.claim_text: WHO assesses the public health risk as low.
claims.evidence_text: Dr Tedros said, “While this is a serious incident, WHO assesses the public health risk as low.”

claims.claim_text: Five of the eight cases have been confirmed as hantavirus.
claims.evidence_text: Five of the 8 cases have been confirmed as hantavirus.

entities.name: Andes virus
entities.description: The only species known to be capable of limited transmission between humans.
entities.evidence_text: The hantavirus involved is the Andes virus, the only species known to be capable of limited transmission between humans.

entities.name: World Health Organization
entities.description: The organization coordinating the response to the hantavirus cases.
entities.evidence_text: WHO is coordinating closely with multiple countries under the International Health Regulations.

events.evidence_text: WHO has taken a number of actions since it was notified of the situation on Saturday, 2 May 2026.

metrics.name: number of cases
metrics.evidence_text: Eight cases have been reported so far, including three deaths.

metrics.name: number of deaths
metrics.evidence_text: Eight cases have been reported so far, including three deaths.

metrics.name: diagnostic kits shipped
metrics.evidence_text: WHO has arranged for the shipment of 2500 diagnostic kits from Argentina to laboratories in five countries.

citations.title: WHO’s response to hantavirus cases linked to a cruise ship

source_credibility.reason: The information is provided by the World Health Organization.
